---
title: "Peak2Gene"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-05"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE,message=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo                  = FALSE
                      ,warning              = FALSE
                      ,message              = FALSE
                      ,error                = TRUE
                      ,dev                  = c('png','pdf')
                      ,pdf.options(encoding = "ISOLatin9.enc", useKerning=FALSE, useDingbats=FALSE))

```


A report by **Elyas.Mohammadi**

Here I try to prepare the scATACseq data from three sources. 1- Our in house generated data (Heart and brain) 2- Sten's data (Brain) 3- AmeenM data (Heart)


```{r libraries}

# Changing the Seurat version to 5
.libPaths("/cfs/klemming/projects/supr/naiss2023-6-180/elyasmo/Rloading/4.4.Spatial/")
library(ggplot2)

# install.packages('Seurat')
unloadNamespace("Seurat")  
library(Seurat)

.libPaths("/cfs/klemming/projects/supr/naiss2023-6-180/elyasmo/Rloading/4.4/")

# library(data.table)
library(harmony)

library(ggrepel)
library(ggpubr)
library(patchwork)
# library(clusterProfiler)
# library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(AnnotationHub)
# library(EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86)
library(ggthemes)
library(viridis)
library(dplyr)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(GenomeInfoDb)
library(tidyr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(scales)
library(gridExtra)
library(pheatmap)

library(ggh4x)
axis <- ggh4x::guide_axis_truncated(
  trunc_lower = unit(0, "npc"),
  trunc_upper = unit(1.5, "cm"))

library(Signac)




```


```{r read,fig.width=15,fig.height=20}
ATAC.all <- readRDS("./ATAC.all.int3_2.splitted.Rds")
obj <- ATAC.all$Mural_Fibroblasts

```

```{r region,fig.width=15,fig.height=20}

# nice colors for Organ
organ_cols <- c(Brain = "#3A86FF", Heart = "#2FBF71")
gene="MOXD1"
min.cutoff=0.2

# ensure the piece you plot has Organ and the assays you expect
Idents(obj) <- "Organ"
obj$Organ <- factor(obj$Organ, levels = c("Brain","Heart"))

# we'll use peaks + gene activity (ACTIVITY) for linking
DefaultAssay(obj) <- "Peak.celltype"


links_gr <- LinkPeaks(
  object            = obj,
  peak.assay        = "Peak.celltype",
  expression.assay  = "ACTIVITY",
  genes.use         = gene,   # focus on this gene
  distance          = 1000000,       # search window (tune as needed)
  min.dist          = 2000       # avoid TSS self-links that are too close
)

# helper: make a padded window around a gene symbol
get_gene_window <- function(seu, gene, pad.up = 10000,pad.down=200000) {
  gr <- tryCatch({ LookupGeneCoords(seu, gene = gene) }, error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(gr) || length(gr) == 0) return(NULL)
  gr <- suppressWarnings(reduce(gr))
  Extend(gr, upstream = pad.up, downstream = pad.down)
}

region <- get_gene_window(obj, gene, pad.up =200000, pad.down  = 200000)
if (is.null(region)) stop(sprintf("Gene %s not found in object annotations.", gene))

# --- compute/add links if missing

obj@assays$Peak.celltype@links <- links_gr@assays$Peak.celltype@links

## 1) Pull links and keep those for ABCB1 (optionally filter by score)
links_tbl <- as.data.frame(obj@assays$Peak.celltype@links) %>%
  filter(gene == gene, score >= min.cutoff) %>%
  arrange(desc(score))

# Convert peaks -> GRanges
hl_all <- StringToGRanges(links_tbl$peak)

# Keep only peaks overlapping the plotting region
ov_idx <- GenomicRanges::countOverlaps(hl_all, region) > 0

hl_gr   <- hl_all[ov_idx]
links_k <- links_tbl[ov_idx, , drop = FALSE]

# (optional) order by genomic position
ord <- order(start(hl_gr))
hl_gr   <- hl_gr[ord]
links_k <- links_k[ord, , drop = FALSE]


pad_bp <- 3000L  # tweak to taste

# expand each highlighted peak but keep inside the plotting region
xmin <- pmax(start(hl_gr) - pad_bp, start(reduce(region))[1])
xmax <- pmin(end(hl_gr)   + pad_bp, end(reduce(region))[1])

hl_df <- data.frame(
  xmin  = as.integer(xmin),
  xmax  = as.integer(xmax),
  ymin  = -Inf,
  ymax  =  Inf
  # score = links_k$score
)

```



```{r peak2gene,fig.width=15,fig.height=20}

# --- build panels
# Coverage grouped by Organ; include peaks + links; keep annotation off here
cov_plot <- CoveragePlot(
  object           = obj,
  region           = region,
  group.by         = "Organ",
  tile.size        = 100,
  downsample       = TRUE,
  annotation       = FALSE,     # we'll keep your separate gene panel
  peaks            = FALSE,
  links            = FALSE,
  features         = gene,
  expression.assay = "ACTIVITY"
  # , region.highlight = hl_gr      # <-- shaded bands for linked peaks
) &
  scale_fill_manual(values = organ_cols) &
  ggtitle(gene)

```



```{r plot,fig.width=15,fig.height=20}

# Add dashed boxes on top of your CoveragePlot
cov_plot_dashed <- cov_plot +
  ggplot2::geom_rect(
    data        = hl_df,
    ggplot2::aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill        = NA,
    color       = "grey40",
    linetype    = "dashed",
    linewidth   = 0.2)

# optional: show peak coordinates as a thin strip
peak_plot <- PeakPlot(
  object = obj,
  region = region
) &
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  )

# gene annotation track (readable colours)
gene_plot <- AnnotationPlot(
  object = obj,
  region = region
) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(`+` = "steelblue3", `-` = "seagreen4"), guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c(`+` = "grey15",    `-` = "grey55"),    guide = "none") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  )

# optional links-only panel (handy if you want a separate arc row)
link_plot <- LinkPlot(
  object = obj,
  region = region,min.cutoff = min.cutoff
)+
  scale_color_gradient(low = "orange",high = "red4")

# --- combine (Coverage+Peaks+Genes [+ Links])
# If you prefer everything in one, you can skip link_plot and rely on cov_plot(links=TRUE)
final <- CombineTracks(
  plotlist        = list(cov_plot_dashed, peak_plot, gene_plot, link_plot),
  heights         = c(7, 1, 2, 3),   # tweak to taste
  widths          = c(10, 1),         # main tracks + expression violin column
  expression.plot = NULL              # cov_plot already carries the violin
)

```



```{r save,fig.width=15,fig.height=20}

pdf(paste0("/cfs/klemming/projects/supr/naiss2023-6-180/elyasmo/Projects/Tiantan/Results/Manuscript/Figure2/Mural_Precursors/Mural_Precursors.",gene , ".link.pdf"),width = 7.5, height = 4.5) 
final
dev.off()

```




```{r Correlation.Peak2Gene,fig.width=10,fig.height=8}

RNA.int <- readRDS("./RNA.int_2.splitted.Rds")

library(Matrix)
library(dplyr)
library(Hmisc)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(grid)
library(circlize)
library(ggplotify)

## ---------------- config ----------------
obj_rna    <- RNA.int$Mural_Precursors
DefaultAssay(obj_rna) <- "RNA"


#Tip cells
genes_of_interest <- c("FOXQ1", "FOXF2")


#mural_precursor
genes_of_interest <- c("ZIC1", "ZIC4")

#aECs
genes_of_interest <- c("PKD2", "ABCG2", "PPM1K", "TRIT1", "MFSD2A", "CAP1","RLF","CA5A","SLCO1A2","PYROXD1", "BANP","SLC7A5","SLC38A5", "RBM3","SPEN", "ZBTB17", "SRARP","EPHA2")


#cECs
# genes_of_interest <- c("CROT","ABCB1","SLC25A40","LGMN","GOLGA5","CHGA","PAG1","FABP5")


sample_var <- "orig.ident"
organ_var  <- "Organ"
min_cells  <- 50

## ------------- helpers ------------------

# per-organ pseudobulk (genes x samples) + sample metadata
make_pseudobulk <- function(seu, sample_var = "orig.ident", min_cells = 50) {
  stopifnot(sample_var %in% colnames(seu@meta.data))
  samp <- seu@meta.data[[sample_var]]
  keep_samples <- names(which(table(samp) >= min_cells))
  if (length(keep_samples) == 0L) return(NULL)

  seu2  <- subset(seu, cells = colnames(seu)[samp %in% keep_samples])
  samp2 <- seu2@meta.data[[sample_var]]

  cts <- GetAssayData(seu2, slot = "counts")  # genes × cells
  idx <- split(seq_len(ncol(cts)), samp2)

  pb_counts <- do.call(
    cbind,
    lapply(idx, function(ix) Matrix::rowSums(cts[, ix, drop = FALSE]))
  )
  pb_counts <- Matrix(pb_counts, sparse = TRUE)
  colnames(pb_counts) <- names(idx)

  list(counts = pb_counts)
}

# CPM → log1p
to_logCPM <- function(pb_counts) {
  lib <- Matrix::colSums(pb_counts)
  cpm <- t(t(pb_counts) / pmax(lib, 1)) * 1e6
  log1p(cpm)
}

# rcorr heatmap with values + significance on lower triangle
rcorr_heatmap <- function(logCPM, genes, title_text) {
  present <- intersect(genes, rownames(logCPM))
  missing <- setdiff(genes, rownames(logCPM))
  if (length(present) < 2L) {
    message("Not enough genes to correlate for: ", title_text)
    return(NULL)
  }
  if (length(missing) > 0L) {
    message("Missing genes in ", title_text, ": ", paste(missing, collapse = ", "))
  }

  # samples × genes for rcorr (correlates columns)
  mat <- t(as.matrix(logCPM[present, , drop = FALSE]))

  cor_res <- Hmisc::rcorr(mat, type = "spearman")
  cor_mat <- cor_res$r
  cor_p   <- cor_res$P

  # follow your earlier style:
  cor_mat[is.na(cor_mat)] <- 0
  cor_p[is.nan(cor_p)]    <- 1
  cor_p[is.na(cor_p)]     <- 1
  diag(cor_mat) <- 0  # <- if you prefer color scaling by off-diagonals, set to NA instead

  # keep the gene order you supplied
  ord <- present[match(present, genes, nomatch = 0)]
  cor_mat <- cor_mat[ord, ord, drop = FALSE]
  cor_p   <- cor_p  [ord, ord, drop = FALSE]

  col_fun <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(-1, 0, 1), c("#313695", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B"))


  cell_fun <- function(j, i, x, y, w, h, fill) {
    # lower triangle only
    if (as.numeric(x) <= 1 - as.numeric(y) + 1e-6) {
      grid.rect(x, y, w, h, gp = gpar(fill = fill, col = fill))
      # add value + significance
      if (cor_p[i, j] < 0.01) {
        grid.text(paste0(sprintf("%.2f", cor_mat[i, j]), "**"),
                  x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 8))
      } else if (cor_p[i, j] <= 0.05) {
        grid.text(paste0(sprintf("%.2f", cor_mat[i, j]), "*"),
                  x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 8))
      } else {
        grid.text(sprintf("%.2f", cor_mat[i, j]),
                  x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 8))
      }
    }
  }

  ht <- ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(
    cor_mat,
    rect_gp           = gpar(type = "none"),
    column_dend_side  = "bottom",
    column_title      = title_text,
    name              = "correlation",
    col               = col_fun,
    cell_fun          = cell_fun,
    cluster_rows      = TRUE,
    cluster_columns   = TRUE,
    row_names_side    = "left"
  )

  lgd_list <- list(
    Legend(labels = c("<0.01", "<0.05"), title = "p-value",
           graphics = list(
             function(x, y, w, h) grid.text("**", x = x, y = y),
             function(x, y, w, h) grid.text("*",  x = x, y = y)
           ))
  )

  ComplexHeatmap::draw(ht, annotation_legend_list = lgd_list, ht_gap = unit(1, "cm"))
}

as_plot <- function(ht_obj) as.ggplot(function() ComplexHeatmap::draw(ht_obj, newpage = FALSE))

## ------------- run per organ ----------------

# Brain
obj_brain <- subset(obj_rna, subset = !!as.name(organ_var) == "Brain")
pb_brain  <- make_pseudobulk(obj_brain, sample_var = sample_var, min_cells = min_cells)
ht_brain  <- if (!is.null(pb_brain)) {
  logCPM_brain <- to_logCPM(pb_brain$counts)
  rcorr_heatmap(logCPM_brain, genes_of_interest, "Gene–gene correlation (Brain pseudobulk)")
} else NULL

# Heart
obj_heart <- subset(obj_rna, subset = !!as.name(organ_var) == "Heart")
pb_heart  <- make_pseudobulk(obj_heart, sample_var = sample_var, min_cells = min_cells)
ht_heart  <- if (!is.null(pb_heart)) {
  logCPM_heart <- to_logCPM(pb_heart$counts)
  rcorr_heatmap(logCPM_heart, genes_of_interest, "Gene–gene correlation (Heart pseudobulk)")
} else NULL

# Arrange side-by-side (as ggplots) and/or save
plots <- list()
if (!is.null(ht_brain)) plots$Brain <- as_plot(ht_brain)
if (!is.null(ht_heart)) plots$Heart <- as_plot(ht_heart)

if (length(plots) > 0) {
  panel <- wrap_plots(plots, ncol = length(plots))
  print(panel)
  # pdf("/path/to/pseudobulk_corr_brain_heart.pdf", width = 14, height = 6); panel; dev.off()
}


# plots2 <- plots



pdf("/cfs/klemming/projects/supr/naiss2023-6-180/elyasmo/Projects/Tiantan/Results/Manuscript/Final_refinments/ED2/cECs.pdf",width = 15,height = 6)
wrap_plots(plots,ncol = 2)
dev.off()

pdf("/cfs/klemming/projects/supr/naiss2023-6-180/elyasmo/Projects/Tiantan/Results/Manuscript/Final_refinments/ED2/aECs.pdf",width = 25,height = 10)
wrap_plots(plots,ncol = 2)
dev.off()

pdf("/cfs/klemming/projects/supr/naiss2023-6-180/elyasmo/Projects/Tiantan/Results/Manuscript/Final_refinments/ED2/Mural_precursor.pdf",width = 8,height = 3)
wrap_plots(plots,ncol = 2)
dev.off()

pdf("/cfs/klemming/projects/supr/naiss2023-6-180/elyasmo/Projects/Tiantan/Results/Manuscript/Final_refinments/ED2/Tipcells.pdf",width = 8,height = 3)
wrap_plots(plots,ncol = 2)
dev.off()

```
